title: XLA JIT cache serialize/deserialize not implemented for StableHLO mode
goal: XLACompiler::serializeExecutable and XLACompiler::deserializeExecutable (lib/backend/xla/xla_compiler.cpp) are not wired into the compilation pipeline's JIT cache. serializeExecutable does have a working StableHLO-mode implementation (prints the LLVM module to textual IR) but nothing calls it; deserializeExecutable is a stub that always returns nullptr in StableHLO mode (would need llvm::parseIR()). The XLA backend is disabled by default (ESHKOL_XLA_ENABLED=OFF) and only relevant when built with -DESHKOL_XLA_ENABLED=ON and ESHKOL_XLA_FULL_MLIR, so this has no impact on the default build.
next_actions: Wire serializeExecutable's output into whatever on-disk/in-memory JIT cache the LLVM-direct backend already uses (see stdlib object-cache precedent in project memory: stdlib .bc -> object once), so a repeated XLA compilation of the same StableHLO module can skip recompilation.
Implement deserializeExecutable using llvm::parseIR() to reconstruct the llvm::Module* from the cached textual IR.
Add a cache-hit/cache-miss test under the ESHKOL_XLA_ENABLED build configuration (CI XLA lane) verifying a second compile of the same module is faster and produces identical results.
acceptance: Building with -DESHKOL_XLA_ENABLED=ON -DESHKOL_XLA_FULL_MLIR, a repeated compilation of the same StableHLO module hits the cache instead of recompiling.
No behavior change for the default (XLA-disabled) build.
No behavior change for the LLVM-direct (non-StableHLO) XLA mode, which already correctly no-ops both functions.
verification: run the XLA CI lane's existing test suite plus the new cache-hit test
ux_payoff: Compile-time win for XLA-backend users recompiling the same model repeatedly (e.g. iterative REPL workflows); zero impact on the default, non-XLA build most users run.
